mappings.rationaleAI: Provides the engineering foundations underlying automobile design (C.4), ship architecture (F.2), and aircraft configuration (G.1).
Addresses the infrastructure that transportation depends on: roads and highways (B), railroad bridges and tunnels (A.4.a), and canals and inland waterways (F.3).
Traces how steam power transformed transportation, from Stephenson's early locomotives and the spread of railways to the first ocean-crossing steamships (A.4).
Navigational science is inseparable from water (F) and air (G) transportation, and this volume explains the instruments and methods that made long-distance travel possible.
The physics of fluid flow governs both ship hull design (F.2.a, hydrodynamic and hydrostatic factors) and aircraft flight characteristics (G.1).
Examines the propulsion technologies—steam generators, internal-combustion engines, gas turbines, and nuclear reactors—that power ships and other vehicles (F.2.c).
Bridges, tunnels, and harbour structures are essential transportation infrastructure, directly relevant to A.4.a (railroad bridges and tunnels), A.5.a (road bridges), and F.4 (harbour works).
Exploration drove the earliest advances in shipping, from sails and oars (A.3) through the voyages that expanded maritime technology worldwide.
Military aviation accelerated fixed-wing aircraft development, tracing the arc from the Wright brothers' first flights to modern heavier-than-air craft (G.1.b).
Traces the evolution of naval vessels from wooden warships to modern carriers, illuminating ship types (F.1), shipbuilding (F.2.b), and the historical arc of maritime technology.
Urban transportation networks—subways, streetcars, bus systems—are central to A.5.b (mass urban transport and traffic networks).
The ancient overland trade routes illustrate A.2 (the wheel and the road), showing how transportation technology and commerce co-evolved over millennia.
Industrial design shapes how vehicles look and function, directly relevant to C.4 (automobile design) and the broader aesthetics of transportation equipment.
Addresses the environmental footprint of transportation systems and the push toward sustainable alternatives in A.5 (development of modern transport).
Transportation revolutions—canals, railways, steamships, air routes—reshaped global trade, spanning A (history of transportation) from primitive methods to modern air transport.
